title: Basis of the subquotient R^{k,l,m}/R^{k,l,m-1} via column-height filtration

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Load-bearing lemma (S1). Produce an explicit Q-basis of the subalgebra R^{k,l,m}=Q<e_1,...,e_m> inside R^{k,l}=Q[e_1,...,e_k]/(h_{l+1},...,h_{l+k}), and of the quotient R^{k,l,m}/R^{k,l,m-1}, with controlled degrees.

Claim: define F_m = span{ s_lambda : lambda in (l^k) box, lambda'_1 <= m } (longest column height <= m). Then F_m = R^{k,l,m}, hence R^{k,l,m}/R^{k,l,m-1} has basis { s_lambda : lambda in (l^k), lambda'_1 = m } and Hilb = sum_{such lambda} q^{|lambda|}.

Two inclusions, both nontrivial:
(a) R^{k,l,m} subset F_m: e_i = s_{(1^i)}; by Pieri, multiplying by e_i (i<=m) never creates a Schur class with a column taller than m, and box truncation only deletes terms (preserves the column bound).
(b) F_m subset R^{k,l,m}: by dual Jacobi-Trudi s_lambda = det(e_{lambda'_i - i + j}); for lambda'_1 <= m this is a polynomial in e_1,...,e_m. Must show the relations (h_{l+1},...,h_{l+k}) do NOT obstruct realizing each such s_lambda inside the subalgebra (reverse induction on |lambda|).

WARNING: Q[e_1,...,e_m] -> R^{k,l} is NOT injective, so one cannot just restrict the box-monomial basis; both linear independence AND spanning must be argued inside the subalgebra. Numerically the resulting Hilbert series matches the target for all tested (k,l,m).
